blocking_issues:
check-verdict failed (exit 1):
Review verdict: FAIL — 1 blocking issue(s):
  • RC-1: Reducer is not a pure function — stream.system_notice case (reducer.ts:327) generates blockId via `Date.now()` and `Math.random()`, both of which are side effects that make the function non-deterministic. Calling applyFrame with the same state and same frame twice produces different blockIds. Fix: replace with a deterministic ID derived from frame.seq (available on every ServerFrame), e.g. `notice-${sid}-${frame.seq}`.

entries.note: Fixed the sole blocking issue from attempt 2: the stream.system_notice case in applyFrame was generating blockId with Date.now() and Math.random(), making the reducer non-deterministic. Replaced with `notice-${sid}-${frame.seq}` — fully deterministic since seq is present on every ServerFrame. Added a regression test 'stream.system_notice produces the same blockId when called twice with the same frame (determinism)' to tests/web/reducer.test.ts to lock in the invariant. All 29 reducer unit tests pass; full suite (1093 tests) green.
known_risks: If two stream.system_notice frames arrive for the same session with the same seq value (e.g. a backfill replay), they would produce the same blockId and the second would overwrite the first in the ring. This is consistent with the deduplication invariant (seq <= hwm is dropped by the WS client) so in practice it should not occur, but reviewers should verify seq uniqueness is guaranteed by the protocol.
